channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SANDF to support SAPS in targeted operations against gangs and illegal mining
item.description: Acting Police Minister Firoz Cachalia has clarified that the deployment of the South African National Defence Force (SANDF) is not a quick fix for the country's crime challenges. Addressing a joint me...

item.title: OpenAI to allow Canadian experts to review safety protocols after Tumbler Ridge tragedy
item.description: Following the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., Canada's Artificial Intelligence Minister, Evan Solomon, held a virtual meeting with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman to address safety concerns around ChatGPT...

item.title: Study Shows Bungoma Youth Lack Awareness on Climate Justice
item.description: A recent study by the Youth Initiative for Land in Africa (YILAA), supported by the Youth Climate Justice Fund, reveals that the majority of young people in Bungoma County have limited knowledge of cl...

item.title: PNP to Study Feasibility of Electric Vehicles for Patrol Fleet Due to Rising Fuel Prices from Middle East Tensions
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) announced on Thursday, March 5, 2026, that it is seriously considering the adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) as part of its patrol car fleet. This comes in respo...

item.title: Senator Bong Go provides relief to fire-hit families in Mandaluyong
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Senator Bong Go extended aid to families displaced by a recent fire in Barangay Addition Hills, Mandaluyong. The fire, which affected densely populated areas, left many families ...

item.title: Ashmolean Museum to Return 16th-Century Bronze Statue of Saint Tirumankai Alvar to Tamil Nadu
item.description: The Ashmolean Museum in Oxford is preparing to return a 500-year-old bronze statue of Saint Tirumankai Alvar, a South Indian Hindu saint, to Tamil Nadu, India. The 57.5cm tall statue was originally ho...

item.title: Harry Maguire receives suspended sentence over 2020 Mykonos case
item.description: Harry Maguire, the Manchester United centre-back, has been handed a 15-month suspended sentence by a Greek court following an incident on the island of Mykonos in August 2020. He was convicted of non-...

item.title: Thane man arrested for sexually assaulting minor girl over six months and extorting money through blackmail
item.description: In Thane, Maharashtra, a 23-year-old man named Prathamesh Desai, who worked as a delivery partner for a local courier company and resided in Lokmanya Nagar, was arrested by the Vartak Nagar Police on ...

item.title: Liberal Party Election Review on 2025 Loss Released in Parliament
item.description: When the Liberal Party received a critical review of its disastrous 2025 federal election campaign, the party’s federal executive initially tried to suppress the findings. The report highlighted serio...

item.title: Tanzania Issues Safety Advisory for Citizens in the Middle East Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The government of Tanzania has released an urgent travel and security advisory for its citizens living in or planning trips to the Middle East, including countries like Bahrain, Israel, Kuwait, Qatar,...

item.title: Police Internal Report Reveals Unlawful Arrest, Torture and Frame-Up of Enugu Businessman in Land Dispute Case
item.description: One internal investigation report from di Nigeria Police Force Complaint Response Unit (CRU) don expose serious misconduct by officers from di Force Intelligence Department’s Intelligence Response Tea...

item.title: Pakistan Urges Enhanced Economic Cooperation and Modernisation of SCO at Council of Heads of Government Meeting in Moscow
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar represented Pakistan at the 24th meeting of the SCO Council of Heads of Government (CHG) in Moscow, which serves as the key body for decisions on e...

item.title: The Liberals’ Reluctance to Release Election-Related Reviews
item.description: The article highlights concerns over the Liberal Party’s apparent lack of transparency regarding internal reviews, particularly relating to elections. Beyond the formal election review, there are indi...

item.title: Investment Boost in Njombe Region Ahead of UDOM Campus Launch
item.description: Njombe Region is seeing a strong surge of investment as businesses prepare for the opening of the University of Dodoma (UDOM) Njombe Campus. New commercial buildings, hostels, banks, and restaurants a...

item.title: Climate Change Alters Snow Distribution Across Canada
item.description: Canada's snowpack, a critical natural reservoir for rivers, agriculture, ecosystems, and hydropower, is undergoing significant changes due to warming winters. Traditionally measured using snow water e...

item.title: Women’s rights groups file ethics complaint vs. Rep. Bong Suntay for inappropriate remark about Anne Curtis
item.description: Women’s rights advocates filed an ethics complaint against Quezon City Representative Bong Suntay following his controversial comment about actress Anne Curtis. The complaint was filed on Thursday, ac...

item.title: PTI founder Imran Khan’s sisters briefly detained during Rawalpindi protest
item.description: In Rawalpindi, the sisters of Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) founder – Aleema Khan, Noreen Khan Niazi, and Uzma Khanum – were briefly taken into police custody during a protest outside Central Jail. T...

item.title: Bureau of Corrections re-arrests Sablayan Prison escapee in Sta. Lucia
item.description: The Bureau of Corrections (BuCor) reported the re-arrest of a person deprived of liberty (PDL) who escaped from the Sablayan Prison and Penal Farm. The PDL was apprehended early Thursday morning at ar...

item.title: Tennis Star Aryna Sabalenka Gets Engaged to Oakberry CEO Georgios Frangulis
item.description: Belarusian tennis world number one Aryna Sabalenka has announced her engagement to Brazilian entrepreneur Georgios Frangulis after months of playful hints. The couple, who began dating in early 2024 a...

item.title: China Announces Lowest Economic Growth Target in Decades, Citing Domestic and Global Challenges
item.description: China has set its lowest economic growth target in decades, aiming for 4.5 to 5 per cent growth this year. Premier Li Qiang outlined the target during the opening of the National People’s Congress, ci...
